heroHeadline: FIFA Congress Vancouver 4 Days Out — Iran Decision Looms; Gnabry Ruled Out; Final Tickets Hit $2.3M
heroSubtitle: The 76th FIFA Congress convenes in Vancouver on April 30 — four days away — as the formal decision point for Iran's World Cup participation. US Secretary of State Marco Rubio's April 24 statement cleared Iranian players to attend ('Nothing from the US has told them they can't come') but drew a hard IRGC red line, raising urgent visa questions for captain Mehdi Taremi and other squad members who completed mandatory Revolutionary Guard service. Iran Football Federation president Mehdi Taj confirmed replacement players have been identified. Separately, Germany's Serge Gnabry confirmed on April 22 that his World Cup dream is 'sadly over' after an adductor muscle tear — deepening Germany's Group E squad concerns alongside Leroy Sané's injury. In the ticket market, CNN reported World Cup Final seats now reselling for $2.3 million each on the secondary market; the cheapest available standard Final ticket is $10,990. The Vancouver Congress will also vote on the 2031 Women's World Cup host — an uncontested joint bid by USA, Mexico, Costa Rica, and Jamaica. Palestine Football Association officials remain barred from attending after Canadian visa denials on April 14. 46 days to kickoff.
footerNote: Data sourced from FIFA.com, UEFA.com, US Soccer, CONCACAF, ESPN, The Athletic, BBC Sport, AP, Reuters, NPR, Al Jazeera, Human Rights Watch, Amnesty International, American Immigration Council, Middle East Monitor, CBS Sports, Bloomberg, Fortune, UPI, CNN, CNBC, NBC Dallas-Fort Worth, Fox 5 New York, Inside World Football, CBC Sports, Johnson County Post, PR Newswire, Yahoo Sports, RTE Sport, TRT World, Time, Xinhua, The National, HuffPost, Washington Post, Washington Times, Canada Soccer, DFB (German Football Association)
